stress.where: The word 'circuit' and its faithfulness metrics (IOI, ACDC, edge attribution patching; 'Transformer Circuit Faithfulness Metrics Are Not Robust' arXiv:2407.08734; 'Many Circuits, One Mechanism' arXiv:2606.06267)
stress.why: A circuit is defined as 'the subgraph that faithfully implements the task', but measured faithfulness swings wildly with methodological choices that should be irrelevant (zero vs mean vs resample ablation, which token positions, choice of corrupted distribution); different discovery methods on the same task return different subgraphs, each 'faithful' under its own metric; and multiple disjoint subgraphs can score equally faithful for one behavior. The concept silently conflates 'sufficient component set under intervention distribution D' with 'the algorithm the model runs'. Practitioners patch with extra criteria (minimality, completeness) instead of revising the concept.

concept.name: Intervention-Indexed Circuit Family (IICF)
concept.definition: A circuit for task T is not a subgraph but a map C: spec -> subgraph, where spec ranges over a preregistered grid of intervention choices (ablation in {zero, mean, resample} x corruption distribution in {ABC-style, random-name, unrelated-text} x token-position set x faithfulness threshold tau). C(spec) = the minimal subgraph reaching faithfulness >= tau under that spec. Two derived objects are the only reportable summaries: CORE = intersection of C(spec) over the grid, HULL = union. Any sentence of the form 'the circuit for T' is ill-formed unless quantified over the grid; the swings documented in arXiv:2407.08734 become coordinates of the object rather than noise around it.
concept.prediction: On GPT-2-small IOI with a 3x3x2 spec grid run through existing ACDC/EAP tooling (<1 GPU-day): (a) CORE edges show single-edge ablation necessity (mean logit-diff drop) at least 2x that of edges appearing in exactly one C(spec); (b) CORE edges recur in a fine-tuned or differently-seeded model at a strictly higher rate than the edges of any single-spec circuit. If CORE is empty or its edges are no more necessary/transferable than spec-unique edges, the representation earns nothing and is falsified.
concept.reduces_to: Union/intersection circuits and the sensitivity-analysis appendices of 'Transformer Circuit Faithfulness Metrics Are Not Robust' (arXiv:2407.08734); Woodward-style intervention-relative causation made explicit.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE run (maevtica commit 1e82498; PREREG.md sha256 d538963..., unchanged after). Model A=gpt2, IOI 16 prompts. Edge graph: 157 upstream nodes (embed, 144 heads, 12 MLPs) x 157 downstream inputs (per-head attn_in, mlp_in, logits) = 11,611 valid edges. Circuit finder: EAP, C(spec)=top-k |score|. Spec grid 3x3x2=18: corruption {ABC, SWAP, MEAN} x k {50,100,200} x metric {logit-diff, logprob(IO)}. CORE=∩18 circuits; UNIQUE=edges in exactly 1. Decision rules: (a) REFUTED if CORE empty; SUPPORTED_a iff mean single-edge ablation drop(CORE) >= 2x max(mean drop(UNIQUE),0) AND >= 0.05, using ONE fixed intervention (ABC corruption patch at edge, drop in mean logit-diff), all 15 CORE edges + 40 random UNIQUE (seed 0). (b) Model B = stanford-crfm/alias-gpt2-small-x21 (differently-seeded GPT-2-small); gate B acc>=0.75; R_B=union of B's 18 circuits; SUPPORTED_b iff rate(CORE in R_B) STRICTLY exceeds rate(C_A(s) in R_B) for every one of the 18 specs. Overall SUPPORTED iff both. Files: research_cycle/experiments/iicf_ioi/{PREREG.md,exp.py,result.json,run.log}, results commit 64252d3. Wall clock 282s, CPU only.
test.numbers: CORE=15 edges (embed->a0.10, embed->m0, and the canonical IOI name-mover/backup cluster: a9.6/a9.9/a10.0/a10.1/a10.6/a10.7/a10.10/a11.10 -> logits + inter-head edges); UNIQUE=118, union=449. (a) mean drop CORE=0.286 vs UNIQUE=0.0059 -> ~48x (needed 2x; medians 0.225 vs 0.0097). (b) B does IOI (LD 5.97, acc 1.0); rate(CORE in R_B)=0.533 > max single-spec rate=0.46 (abc,50,lp), strictly above all 18 specs (range 0.21-0.46). Both preregistered conditions met -> SUPPORTED.
test.sanity: Oracle passed on all gates: model A clean logit-diff 3.889 (gate >1.0), accuracy 1.0 (gate >0.9); patching the single top-|EAP| edge a9.9->logits (independently the known IOI name-mover head) dropped logit-diff by 3.446 (gate >=0.3) — the edge-patch machinery demonstrably moves logits hard. Non-gating check: Spearman(|EAP score|, |ablation drop|)=0.85 over the 55 tested edges, so EAP scores track real causal effect. Face validity: CORE recovered the textbook IOI circuit without being told about it.

concept.name: Mechanism Type vs Circuit Token
concept.definition: Treat any particular faithful subgraph as a TOKEN; the reportable discovery is the TYPE. Two faithful subgraphs G1, G2 for task T are tokens of the same mechanism type iff there exists a variable alignment (e.g., found by distributed alignment search) between their intermediate activations such that cross-subgraph interchange interventions -- patch the value of G1's aligned variable into G2's site on counterfactual pairs -- move the output with interchange-intervention accuracy >= kappa (e.g., 0.8). The mechanism type = the equivalence class of subgraphs plus the shared causal-variable graph. 'Many disjoint faithful subgraphs' stops being a paradox: they are multiple tokens of one type (or, informatively, of different types).
concept.prediction: For the disjoint equally-faithful subgraphs of 'Many Circuits, One Mechanism' (arXiv:2606.06267): cross-subgraph interchange accuracy for the task's key intermediate variable exceeds 0.8, while alignments to a mismatched task's variable stay near chance. Falsified if disjoint faithful subgraphs for the same behavior systematically FAIL cross-alignment (then they implement genuinely different algorithms and the type collapses back to tokens).
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / DAS (Geiger et al.) plus the informal 'universality / circuit motif' talk -- the new content is making the equivalence class, not the subgraph, the unit of publication.

test.design: Falsification of "Mechanism Type vs Circuit Token" on gpt2-small IOI (all files in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/mech_type_ioi/: PREREG.md, exp.py, result.json; prereg committed 1c22b59 BEFORE run 1, amendment d46be57 BEFORE run 2). Two DISJOINT name-mover subgraphs, each independently faithful for IOI: S1={9.9, 9.0, 10.2, 10.1}, S2={9.6, 10.0, 10.10, 10.6, 11.2, 9.7} (amendment 1: split rebalanced by faithfulness ONLY after run 1's layer-split left S2 unfaithful at 0.375; cross metric never consulted; thresholds untouched). Key intermediate variable = IO-name identity in each subgraph's aggregate END-position write. Alignment of the variable in subgraph S = top-8 SVD subspace of its base-vs-roleswap displacement vectors. Cross-subgraph interchange = apply S's alignment to the OTHER subgraph's write (delta added at blocks.11.hook_resid_post, END; one intervention type for all 12 conditions). Controls: mismatched-task subspace (greater-than year variable) and random 8-dim subspace. PREREGISTERED RULE: BROKEN_MEASUREMENT if clean acc or full-resid oracle < 0.9; INCONCLUSIVE if either subgraph's full interchange < 0.8 (premise not instantiated) or own-subspace < 0.7 (estimator too weak); SUPPORTED iff min(cross) >= 0.8 AND max(controls) <= 0.2; REFUTED iff qualifications pass AND min(cross) < 0.5.
test.numbers: Run 2 (decisive): clean_acc=1.000; oracle_full_resid=1.000; S1_full=1.000, S2_full=1.000 (both disjoint subgraphs equally faithful — premise instantiated); own-subspace S1=1.000, S2=0.875 (estimator adequate); CROSS S2-subspace-on-S1=0.0625, S1-subspace-on-S2=0.000 — indistinguishable from controls (mismatched-task=0.000/0.000, random=0.000/0.000). Even non-gating full-span k=16 cross stays <=0.125. Geometry: per-pair cos(d_S1,d_S2)=0.30; subspace overlap S1-S2=0.29 vs S1-vs-mismatched-task=0.23 — the two subgraphs' variable codings are nearly as dissimilar as codings of a DIFFERENT task; the only shared part is the rank-1 output readout (~0.47 of each displacement's norm lies along W_U[b]-W_U[a]). Wall clock ~55 s/run, CPU only. Run 1 archived (result_run1.json): INCONCLUSIVE_regime_not_reproduced (backup-mover-only subgraph carried the variable at just 0.375 in the clean run — itself evidence that 'equally faithful disjoint subgraphs' are hard to instantiate from clean-run activity).
test.sanity: Oracle chain all passed: clean IOI accuracy 1.000 and swap-prompt accuracy 1.000 (model does the task); full-residual END interchange flips 16/16 (patching machinery moves logits, oracle=1.000 vs required 0.9); no-op floor 0.000; own-subspace interchange 1.000/0.875 proves the SVD alignment estimator itself causally works when applied to the subgraph it was learned from — so the near-zero cross numbers are a real property of the codings, not a broken measurement or a dead estimator. Random and mismatched controls at 0.000 confirm the flip metric isn't trivially triggerable.
